format_source.description: This sets the data type of the output dataset(s) to be the same format as that of the specified tool input.
format_source.title: format_source

metadata_source.description: This copies the metadata information from the tool’s input dataset to serve as default for information that cannot be detected from the output. One prominent use case is interval data with a non-standard column order that cannot be deduced from a header line, but which is known to be identical in the input and output datasets.
metadata_source.title: metadata_source

from_work_dir.description: Relative path to a file produced by the tool in its working directory. Output’s contents are set to this file’s contents.
from_work_dir.title: from_work_dir

items.description: Provide Javascript/ECMAScript 5.1 code here that will be available for expressions inside the `shell_command` field.
items.title: expression_lib

RegexParameterValidatorModel.description:
Check if a regular expression **matches** the value, i.e. appears
at the beginning of the value. To enforce a match of the complete value use
``$`` at the end of the expression. The expression is given is the content
of the validator tag. Note that for ``selects`` each option is checked
separately.
RegexParameterValidatorModel.title: RegexParameterValidatorModel

cores_min.description:
Minimum reserved number of CPU cores.
May be a fractional value to indicate to a scheduling algorithm that one core can be allocated to multiple jobs. For example, a value of 0.25 indicates that up to 4 jobs may run in parallel on 1 core. A value of 1.25 means that up to 3 jobs can run on a 4 core system (4/1.25 ≈ 3).
The reported number of CPU cores reserved for the process is a non-zero integer calculated by rounding up the cores request to the next whole number.
cores_min.title: cores_min

cores_max.description:
Maximum reserved number of CPU cores.
May be a fractional value to indicate to a scheduling algorithm that one core can be allocated to multiple jobs. For example, a value of 0.25 indicates that up to 4 jobs may run in parallel on 1 core. A value of 1.25 means that up to 3 jobs can run on a 4 core system (4/1.25 ≈ 3).
The reported number of CPU cores reserved for the process is a non-zero integer calculated by rounding up the cores request to the next whole number.
cores_max.title: cores_max

ram_min.description:
Minimum reserved RAM in mebibytes (2**20).
May be a fractional value. If so, the actual RAM request is rounded up to the next whole number. The reported amount of RAM reserved for the process is a non-zero integer.
ram_min.title: ram_min

ram_max.description:
Maximum reserved RAM in mebibytes (2**20).
May be a fractional value. If so, the actual RAM request is rounded up to the next whole number. The reported amount of RAM reserved for the process is a non-zero integer.
ram_max.title: ram_max

timelimit.description: Maximum time in seconds the tool is allowed to run. Job will be terminated if exceeded.
timelimit.title: timelimit

YamlToolTest.description:
In-tool test case as authored in YAML tool fixtures.

Mirrors ``TestJob`` (the workflow test schema) but uses ``inputs``
instead of ``job`` and adds tool-specific assertion fields
(``assert_stdout``, ``assert_stderr``, ``command``, etc.).  Output
assertions reuse the same ``TestOutputAssertions`` union so the
typing is shared between workflow tests and tool tests.
YamlToolTest.title: YamlToolTest

attribute_is_model.description:
Asserts the XML ``attribute`` for the element (or tag) with the specified
XPath-like ``path`` is the specified ``text``.

For example:

```xml
<attribute_is path="outerElement/innerElement1" attribute="foo" text="bar" />
```

The assertion implicitly also asserts that an element matching ``path`` exists.
With ``negate`` the result of the assertion (on the equality) can be inverted (the
implicit assertion on the existence of the path is not affected).

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

attribute.description: The XML attribute name to test against from the target XML element.
attribute.title: Attribute

text.description: The expected attribute value to test against on the target XML element
text.title: Text

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

attribute_is_model.title: attribute_is_model

attribute_is_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
attribute_is_model_nested.title: attribute_is_model_nested

attribute_matches_model.description:
Asserts the XML ``attribute`` for the element (or tag) with the specified
XPath-like ``path`` matches the regular expression specified by ``expression``.

For example:

```xml
<attribute_matches path="outerElement/innerElement2" attribute="foo2" expression="bar\d+" />
```

The assertion implicitly also asserts that an element matching ``path`` exists.
With ``negate`` the result of the assertion (on the matching) can be inverted (the
implicit assertion on the existence of the path is not affected).

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

attribute.description: The XML attribute name to test against from the target XML element.
attribute.title: Attribute

expression.description: The regular expressions to apply against the named attribute on the target XML element.
expression.title: Expression

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

attribute_matches_model.title: attribute_matches_model

attribute_matches_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
attribute_matches_model_nested.title: attribute_matches_model_nested

element_text_is_model.description:
Asserts the text of the XML element with the specified XPath-like ``path`` is
the specified ``text``.

For example:

```xml
<element_text_is path="BlastOutput_program" text="blastp" />
```

The assertion implicitly also asserts that an element matching ``path`` exists.
With ``negate`` the result of the assertion (on the equality) can be inverted (the
implicit assertion on the existence of the path is not affected).

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

text.description: The expected element text (body of the XML tag) to test against on the target XML element
text.title: Text

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

element_text_is_model.title: element_text_is_model

element_text_is_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
element_text_is_model_nested.title: element_text_is_model_nested

element_text_matches_model.description:
Asserts the text of the XML element with the specified XPath-like ``path``
matches the regular expression defined by ``expression``.

For example:

```xml
<element_text_matches path="BlastOutput_version" expression="BLASTP\s+2\.2.*"/>
```

The assertion implicitly also asserts that an element matching ``path`` exists.
With ``negate`` the result of the assertion (on the matching) can be inverted (the
implicit assertion on the existence of the path is not affected).

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

expression.description: The regular expressions to apply against the target element.
expression.title: Expression

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

element_text_matches_model.title: element_text_matches_model

element_text_matches_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
element_text_matches_model_nested.title: element_text_matches_model_nested

element_text_model.description:
This tag allows the developer to recurisively specify additional assertions as
child elements about just the text contained in the element specified by the
XPath-like ``path``, e.g.

```xml
<element_text path="BlastOutput_iterations/Iteration/Iteration_hits/Hit/Hit_def">
  <not_has_text text="EDK72998.1" />
</element_text>
```

The assertion implicitly also asserts that an element matching ``path`` exists.
With ``negate`` the result of the implicit assertions can be inverted.
The sub-assertions, which have their own ``negate`` attribute, are not affected
by ``negate``.

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

element_text_model.title: element_text_model

element_text_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
element_text_model_nested.title: element_text_model_nested

has_archive_member_model.description:
This tag allows to check if ``path`` is contained in a compressed file.

The path is a regular expression that is matched against the full paths of the objects in
the compressed file (remember that "matching" means it is checked if a prefix of
the full path of an archive member is described by the regular expression).
Valid archive formats include ``.zip``, ``.tar``, and ``.tar.gz``. Note that
depending on the archive creation method:

- full paths of the members may be prefixed with ``./``
- directories may be treated as empty files

```xml
<has_archive_member path="./path/to/my-file.txt"/>
```

With ``n`` and ``delta`` (or ``min`` and ``max``) assertions on the number of
archive members matching ``path`` can be expressed. The following could be used,
e.g., to assert an archive containing n±1 elements out of which at least
4 need to have a ``txt`` extension.

```xml
<has_archive_member path=".*" n="10" delta="1"/>
<has_archive_member path=".*\.txt" min="4"/>
```

In addition the tag can contain additional assertions as child elements about
the first member in the archive matching the regular expression ``path``. For
instance

```xml
<has_archive_member path=".*/my-file.txt">
  <not_has_text text="EDK72998.1"/>
</has_archive_member>
```

If the ``all`` attribute is set to ``true`` then all archive members are subject
to the assertions. Note that, archive members matching the ``path`` are sorted
alphabetically.

The ``negate`` attribute of the ``has_archive_member`` assertion only affects
the asserts on the presence and number of matching archive members, but not any
sub-assertions (which can offer the ``negate`` attribute on their own).  The
check if the file is an archive at all, which is also done by the function, is
not affected.

path.description: The regular expression specifying the archive member.
path.title: Path

all.description: Check the sub-assertions for all paths matching the path. Default: false, i.e. only the first
all.title: All

n.description: Desired number, can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
n.title: N

delta.description: Allowed difference with respect to n (default: 0), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_archive_member_model.title: has_archive_member_model

has_archive_member_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_archive_member_model_nested.title: has_archive_member_model_nested

has_element_with_path_model.description:
Asserts the XML output contains at least one element (or tag) with the specified
XPath-like ``path``, e.g.

```xml
<has_element_with_path path="BlastOutput_param/Parameters/Parameters_matrix" />
```

With ``negate`` the result of the assertion can be inverted.

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_element_with_path_model_nested.title: has_element_with_path_model_nested

has_h5_attribute_model.description:
Asserts HDF5 output contains the specified ``value`` for an attribute (``key``), e.g.

```xml
<has_h5_attribute key="nchroms" value="15" />
```

key.description: HDF5 attribute to check value of.
key.title: Key

value.description: Expected value of HDF5 attribute to check.
value.title: Value

has_image_center_of_mass_model.description:
Asserts the specified output is an image and has the specified center of mass.

Asserts the output is an image and has a specific center of mass,
or has an Euclidean distance of ``eps`` or less to that point (e.g.,
``<has_image_center_of_mass center_of_mass="511.07, 223.34" />``).

center_of_mass.description: The required center of mass of the image intensities (horizontal and vertical coordinate, separated by a comma).
center_of_mass.title: Center Of Mass

channel.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific channel of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image channel).
channel.title: Channel

slice.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific slice of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image slice).
slice.title: Slice

frame.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific frame of the image sequence (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image frame).
frame.title: Frame

eps.description: The maximum allowed Euclidean distance to the required center of mass (defaults to ``0.01``).
eps.title: Eps

has_image_center_of_mass_model.title: has_image_center_of_mass_model

has_image_center_of_mass_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_center_of_mass_model_nested.title: has_image_center_of_mass_model_nested

has_image_channels_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has a specific number of channels.

The number of channels is plus/minus ``delta`` (e.g., ``<has_image_channels channels="3" />``).

Alternatively the range of the expected number of channels can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

channels.description: Expected number of channels of the image.
channels.title: Channels

delta.description: Maximum allowed difference of the number of channels (default is 0). The observed number of channels has to be in the range ``value +- delta``.
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed number of channels.
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed number of channels.
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_image_channels_model.title: has_image_channels_model

has_image_channels_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_channels_model_nested.title: has_image_channels_model_nested

has_image_depth_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has a specific depth (number of slices).

The depth is plus/minus ``delta`` (e.g., ``<has_image_depth depth="512" delta="2" />``).
Alternatively the range of the expected depth can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

depth.description: Expected depth of the image (number of slices).
depth.title: Depth

delta.description: Maximum allowed difference of the image depth (number of slices, default is 0). The observed depth has to be in the range ``value +- delta``.
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed depth of the image (number of slices).
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed depth of the image (number of slices).
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_image_depth_model.title: has_image_depth_model

has_image_depth_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_depth_model_nested.title: has_image_depth_model_nested

has_image_frames_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has a specific number of frames (number of time steps).

The number of frames is plus/minus ``delta`` (e.g., ``<has_image_frames depth="512" delta="2" />``).
Alternatively the range of the expected number of frames can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

frames.description: Expected number of frames in the image sequence (number of time steps).
frames.title: Frames

delta.description: Maximum allowed difference of the number of frames in the image sequence (number of time steps, default is 0). The observed number of frames has to be in the range ``value +- delta``.
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed number of frames in the image sequence (number of time steps).
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed number of frames in the image sequence (number of time steps).
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_image_frames_model.title: has_image_frames_model

has_image_frames_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_frames_model_nested.title: has_image_frames_model_nested

has_image_height_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has a specific height (in pixels).

The height is plus/minus ``delta`` (e.g., ``<has_image_height height="512" delta="2" />``).
Alternatively the range of the expected height can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

height.description: Expected height of the image (in pixels).
height.title: Height

delta.description: Maximum allowed difference of the image height (in pixels, default is 0). The observed height has to be in the range ``value +- delta``.
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed height of the image (in pixels).
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed height of the image (in pixels).
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_image_height_model.title: has_image_height_model

has_image_height_model_nested.title: has_image_height_model_nested

has_image_mean_intensity_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has a specific mean intensity value.

The mean intensity value is plus/minus ``eps`` (e.g., ``<has_image_mean_intensity mean_intensity="0.83" />``).
Alternatively the range of the expected mean intensity value can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

channel.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific channel of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image channel).
channel.title: Channel

slice.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific slice of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image slice).
slice.title: Slice

frame.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific frame of the image sequence (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image frame).
frame.title: Frame

mean_intensity.description: The required mean value of the image intensities.
mean_intensity.title: Mean Intensity

eps.description: The absolute tolerance to be used for ``value`` (defaults to ``0.01``). The observed mean value of the image intensities has to be in the range ``value +- eps``.
eps.title: Eps

min.description: A lower bound of the required mean value of the image intensities.
min.title: Min

max.description: An upper bound of the required mean value of the image intensities.
max.title: Max

has_image_mean_intensity_model.title: has_image_mean_intensity_model

has_image_mean_intensity_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_mean_intensity_model_nested.title: has_image_mean_intensity_model_nested

has_image_mean_object_size_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image with labeled objects which have the specified mean size (number of pixels),

The mean size is plus/minus ``eps`` (e.g., ``<has_image_mean_object_size mean_object_size="111.87" exclude_labels="0" />``).

The labels must be unique.

channel.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific channel of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image channel).
channel.title: Channel

slice.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific slice of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image slice).
slice.title: Slice

frame.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific frame of the image sequence (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image frame).
frame.title: Frame

labels.description: List of labels, separated by a comma. Labels *not* on this list will be excluded from consideration. Cannot be used in combination with ``exclude_labels``.
labels.title: Labels

exclude_labels.description: List of labels to be excluded from consideration, separated by a comma. The primary usage of this attribute is to exclude the background of a label image. Cannot be used in combination with ``labels``.
exclude_labels.title: Exclude Labels

mean_object_size.description: The required mean size of the uniquely labeled objects.
mean_object_size.title: Mean Object Size

eps.description: The absolute tolerance to be used for ``value`` (defaults to ``0.01``). The observed mean size of the uniquely labeled objects has to be in the range ``value +- eps``.
eps.title: Eps

min.description: A lower bound of the required mean size of the uniquely labeled objects.
min.title: Min

max.description: An upper bound of the required mean size of the uniquely labeled objects.
max.title: Max

has_image_mean_object_size_model.title: has_image_mean_object_size_model

has_image_mean_object_size_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_mean_object_size_model_nested.title: has_image_mean_object_size_model_nested

has_image_n_labels_model.description:
Asserts the output is an image and has the specified labels.

Labels can be a number of labels or unique values (e.g.,
``<has_image_n_labels n="187" exclude_labels="0" />``).

The primary usage of this assertion is to verify the number of objects in images with uniquely labeled objects.

channel.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific channel of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image channel).
channel.title: Channel

slice.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific slice of the image (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image slice).
slice.title: Slice

frame.description: Restricts the assertion to a specific frame of the image sequence (where ``0`` corresponds to the first image frame).
frame.title: Frame

labels.description: List of labels, separated by a comma. Labels *not* on this list will be excluded from consideration. Cannot be used in combination with ``exclude_labels``.
labels.title: Labels

exclude_labels.description: List of labels to be excluded from consideration, separated by a comma. The primary usage of this attribute is to exclude the background of a label image. Cannot be used in combination with ``labels``.
exclude_labels.title: Exclude Labels

n.description: Expected number of labels.
n.title: N

delta.description: Maximum allowed difference of the number of labels (default is 0). The observed number of labels has to be in the range ``value +- delta``.
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed number of labels.
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed number of labels.
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_image_n_labels_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_image_n_labels_model_nested.title: has_image_n_labels_model_nested

Asserts the output is an image and has a specific width (in pixels).

The width is plus/minus ``delta`` (e.g., ``<has_image_width width="512" delta="2" />``).
Alternatively the range of the expected width can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

width.title: Width

delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum allowed width of the image (in pixels).
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum allowed width of the image (in pixels).
max.title: Max

negate.title: Negate

Asserts the specified output contains a line matching the
regular expression specified by the argument expression. If n is given
the assertion checks for exactly n occurences.

expression.title: Expression

n.description: Desired number, can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
n.title: N

delta.description: Allowed difference with respect to n (default: 0), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

negate.title: Negate

has_line_model.description:
Asserts the specified output contains the line specified by the
argument line. The exact number of occurrences can be optionally
specified by the argument n

line.description: The full line of text to search for in the output.
line.title: Line

n.description: Desired number, can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
n.title: N

delta.description: Allowed difference with respect to n (default: 0), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

has_n_columns_model.description:
Asserts tabular output  contains the specified
number (``n``) of columns.

For instance, ``<has_n_columns n="3"/>``. The assertion tests only the first line.
Number of columns can optionally also be specified with ``delta``. Alternatively the
range of expected occurences can be specified by ``min`` and/or ``max``.

Optionally a column separator (``sep``, default is ``       ``) `and comment character(s)
can be specified (``comment``, default is empty string). The first non-comment
line is used for determining the number of columns.

n.description: Desired number, can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
n.title: N

delta.description: Allowed difference with respect to n (default: 0), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

comment.description: Comment character(s) used to skip comment lines (which should not be used for counting columns)
comment.title: Comment

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

has_n_columns_model.title: has_n_columns_model

has_n_columns_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
has_n_columns_model_nested.title: has_n_columns_model_nested

has_n_elements_with_path_model.description:
Asserts the XML output contains the specified number (``n``, optionally with ``delta``) of elements (or
tags) with the specified XPath-like ``path``.

For example:

```xml
<has_n_elements_with_path n="9" path="BlastOutput_iterations/Iteration/Iteration_hits/Hit/Hit_num" />
```

Alternatively to ``n`` and ``delta`` also the ``min`` and ``max`` attributes
can be used to specify the range of the expected number of occurences.
With ``negate`` the result of the assertion can be inverted.

delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

xml_element_model.description:
Assert if the XML file contains element(s) or tag(s) with the specified
[XPath-like ``path``](https://lxml.de/xpathxslt.html).  If ``n`` and ``delta``
or ``min`` and ``max`` are given also the number of occurences is checked.

```xml
<assert_contents>
  <xml_element path="./elem"/>
  <xml_element path="./elem/more[2]"/>
  <xml_element path=".//more" n="3" delta="1"/>
</assert_contents>
```

With ``negate="true"`` the outcome of the assertions wrt the precence and number
of ``path`` can be negated. If there are any sub assertions then check them against

- the content of the attribute ``attribute``
- the element's text if no attribute is given

```xml
<assert_contents>
  <xml_element path="./elem/more[2]" attribute="name">
    <has_text_matching expression="foo$"/>
  </xml_element>
</assert_contents>
```

Sub-assertions are not subject to the ``negate`` attribute of ``xml_element``.
If ``all`` is ``true`` then the sub assertions are checked for all occurences.

Note that all other XML assertions can be expressed by this assertion (Galaxy
also implements the other assertions by calling this one).

path.description: The Python xpath-like expression to find the target element.
path.title: Path

attribute.description: The XML attribute name to test against from the target XML element.
attribute.title: Attribute

all.description: Check the sub-assertions for all paths matching the path. Default: false, i.e. only the first
all.title: All

n.description: Desired number, can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
n.title: N

delta.description: Allowed difference with respect to n (default: 0), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
delta.title: Delta

min.description: Minimum number (default: -infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
min.title: Min

max.description: Maximum number (default: infinity), can be suffixed by ``(k|M|G|T|P|E)i?``
max.title: Max

negate.description: A boolean that can be set to true to negate the outcome of the assertion.
negate.title: Negate

xml_element_model.title: xml_element_model

xml_element_model_nested.description: Nested version of this assertion model.
xml_element_model_nested.title: xml_element_model_nested

id.description: Unique identifier for the tool. Should be all lower-case and should not include whitespace.
id.title: id

name.description: The name of the tool, displayed in the tool menu. This is not the same as the tool id, which is a unique identifier for the tool.
name.title: name

description.description: The description is displayed in the tool menu immediately following the hyperlink for the tool.
description.title: description

configfiles.description: A list of config files for this tool.
configfiles.title: configfiles

container.description: Container image to use for this tool.
container.title: container

requirements.description: A list of requirements needed to execute this tool. These can be javascript expressions, resource requirements or container images.
requirements.title: requirements

shell_command.description: A string that contains the command to be executed. Parameters can be referenced inside $().
shell_command.title: shell_command

license.description: A full URI or a a short [SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) identifier for a license for this tool wrapper. The tool wrapper license can be independent of the underlying tool license. This license covers the tool yaml and associated scripts shipped with the tool.
license.title: license

help.description: Help text shown below the tool interface.
help.title: help
